title: Angry Customer - Late Package Delivery
description: A customer is furious because their package has been delayed for 5 days with no updates. They need it for an important event tomorrow.

persona.name: Michelle
persona.goal: Get a refund or guaranteed delivery by tomorrow

background_context: The customer ordered a gift for their spouse's birthday, which is tomorrow. The package was supposed to arrive 5 days ago but tracking shows it's stuck at a distribution center with no updates for 3 days. They've already called twice before with no resolution. They paid for express shipping.
opening_message: I've been waiting for my package for FIVE days now! The tracking hasn't updated in three days and my husband's birthday is TOMORROW. I paid extra for express shipping and this is the third time I'm calling about this. Where is my package?!
success_criteria: Acknowledge the customer's frustration without being defensive
Offer a concrete solution (refund, replacement, or expedited reshipment)
De-escalate the customer's anger to at least 'frustrated' level
Provide clear next steps and timeline
common_mistakes: Making excuses or blaming the shipping carrier
Being defensive when customer expresses anger
Offering solutions without first acknowledging emotions
Using corporate jargon instead of empathetic language
Failing to take ownership of the problem
